message:
refactor(install + health): simplify claude-mcp re-register, convert durability status to exhaustive match

Two unrelated cleanups from the latest review pass:

install.sh — setup_claude_code_mcp_via_cli
-------------------------------------------
The re-register path previously ran `claude mcp list`, grep'd its
output with a hand-rolled regex for `^[[:space:]]*mcp-agent-mail
[:[:space:]]`, and only ran `claude mcp remove` when the regex
matched. This was fragile in two ways:

  - Any change to `claude mcp list` output format (column headers,
    JSON mode, empty-row formatting) would silently make the regex
    stop matching, and the subsequent `claude mcp add` would either
    refuse (duplicate name) or silently leave the stale entry in
    place with its old URL / bearer token.
  - On a fresh install where the config file itself was missing,
    `claude mcp list` exited non-zero into the `|| true`, the list
    came back empty, the regex missed, and then `claude mcp add`
    was invoked anyway — wasting the branch entirely.

Replace with an unconditional best-effort remove-then-add:
  claude mcp remove mcp-agent-mail --scope user 2>/dev/null || true
  claude mcp remove mcp-agent-mail            2>/dev/null || true
  claude mcp add   mcp-agent-mail ...
`claude mcp remove` is a documented no-op when the entry is
absent, so the unconditional call is safe on first install. The
double-try (with and without `--scope user`) handles the
older/newer `claude` CLI versions that disagree on what the
default scope is. Net: 12 lines of regex-gating gone, re-register
is now format-independent.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs — /health status_code
-------------------------------------------------------------
The durability-state branch of the HTTP /health handler matched
DurabilityState via a chain of nested `matches!(...)` expressions.
That worked, but with `Corrupt | Recovering` matched together and
`Healthy` implicit in the outer `else` arm, a future variant
addition (e.g., `Quarantine`) would default to HTTP 200 and fly
under the radar.

Convert to an exhaustive `match` on every DurabilityState variant:
  DurabilityState::Healthy             → 200
  DurabilityState::DegradedReadOnly    → 200 + "ready_degraded"
  DurabilityState::Recovering | Corrupt → 503 + "degraded"

The compiler will now force any new variant through the
match-arm audit at compile time. Also pulls the enum into scope
via `use mcp_agent_mail_db::DurabilityState` so the arms stay
readable.

Also refines the `probe_runtime_durability_state` doc comment:
the previous wording said the function returned None "when the
verdict engine panics, indistinguishable from healthy" — that's
not actually true any more (the engine catches panics via
`std::panic::catch_unwind` and reports them through the state).
The new wording states only the true behavior: :memory: DBs have
no on-disk state to probe, so their durability is None; all
file-backed DBs return `Some(state)`.

files.patch:
@@ -3411,21 +3411,17 @@ setup_claude_code_mcp_via_cli() {
   local desired_url
   desired_url="$(desired_mcp_http_url)"
 
-  # `claude mcp list` exits non-zero if the config is uninitialized on first
-  # run; treat both "missing" and "absent" the same — we'll add.
-  local list_output=""
-  list_output="$(claude mcp list 2>/dev/null || true)"
-
-  # If an entry already exists but points at a different URL or is missing the
-  # Authorization header the current bearer token expects, re-register to keep
-  # the client in sync with the server we just installed.
-  if printf '%s\n' "$list_output" | grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*mcp-agent-mail[:[:space:]]'; then
-    # Best-effort: remove first so the re-add takes effect. `claude mcp remove`
-    # is idempotent and non-fatal if the entry is already gone.
-    claude mcp remove mcp-agent-mail --scope user >/dev/null 2>&1 \
-      || claude mcp remove mcp-agent-mail >/dev/null 2>&1 \
-      || true
-  fi
+  # Best-effort remove first so the add always takes effect with the current
+  # URL + Authorization header. Skipping remove and relying on `add` to update
+  # an existing entry doesn't work — `claude mcp add` refuses to overwrite an
+  # existing name on most `claude` versions, and gating remove on a regex
+  # match against `claude mcp list` output makes us fragile to any future
+  # change to that CLI's listing format. `claude mcp remove` is a no-op when
+  # the entry is absent, so the unconditional call is safe on first install
+  # too; both `|| true` fallbacks swallow the expected non-zero exit code.
+  claude mcp remove mcp-agent-mail --scope user >/dev/null 2>&1 \
+    || claude mcp remove mcp-agent-mail >/dev/null 2>&1 \
+    || true
 
   local -a add_args=(mcp add --scope user --transport http mcp-agent-mail "$desired_url")
   if [ -n "$bearer_token" ]; then
